CREATE TABLE test_users (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(100), email VARCHAR(100), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
CREATE TABLE test_orders (order_id INT PRIMARY KEY, user_id INT, amount DECIMAL(10, 2), order_date TIMESTAMP, FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES test_users(id));
INSERT INTO test_users (id, name, email) VALUES (1, 'Alice', 'alice@example.com');
INSERT INTO test_users (id, name, email) VALUES (2, 'Bob', 'bob@example.com');
INSERT INTO test_orders (order_id, user_id, amount, order_date) VALUES (1, 1, 99.99, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_orders (order_id, user_id, amount, order_date) VALUES (2, 2, 149.99, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
SELECT u.name, o.amount FROM test_users u JOIN test_orders o ON u.id = o.user_id WHERE o.amount > 100;
SELECT u.name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_orders WHERE user_id = u.id) AS order_count FROM test_users u;
SELECT name FROM test_users WHERE email LIKE '%@example.com';
SELECT u.name, u.email, (SELECT SUM(amount) FROM test_orders WHERE user_id = u.id) AS total_spent FROM test_users u ORDER BY total_spent DESC;
UPDATE test_users SET name = 'Alice Smith' WHERE id = 1;
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM test_users WHERE email = 'charlie@example.com') THEN 'Exists' ELSE 'Does Not Exist' END AS email_check;
SELECT u.name, (SELECT MAX(amount) FROM test_orders WHERE user_id = u.id) AS max_order FROM test_users u;
INSERT INTO test_users (id, name, email) VALUES (3, NULL, 'charlie@example.com');
SELECT u.*, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_orders o WHERE o.user_id = u.id) AS order_count FROM test_users u HAVING order_count > 0;
